title: racinaea hasei
intro: Racinaea hasei, also known as the Hase's bromeliad, is a species of bromeliad that belongs to the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Peru and Ecuador. Racinaea hasei has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, and can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use.
